req.title: Standing order
req.statement: A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future; past start dates shall be rejected.
preconditions: authenticated

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two equivalence partitions for start_date: (1) future dates (valid) and (2) past-or-today dates (invalid). BVA identifies today as the exact boundary between the two partitions, making it the critical boundary case. A positive EP test confirms a clearly future date is accepted (HTTP 201 + scheduled status). A negative EP test confirms a clearly past date is rejected (HTTP 422). A BVA test confirms today's date is rejected (HTTP 422), sitting on the invalid boundary. An additional STC test verifies the state transition from 'pending' to 'scheduled' upon successful creation, confirming the order lifecycle begins correctly. A DT test covers the combination of a past date with otherwise valid payload fields to ensure no field-level override can bypass date validation.

tests.name: test_standing_order_future_date_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
start_date is set to today + 30 days (clearly future)
actions: POST /standing-orders with valid payload including start_date = today + 30 days
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-020: future start dates shall be accepted and order scheduled
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_future_date_accepted(auth_fixture, base_url):
    token = auth_fixture
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "payee_id": "<valid_payee_id>",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "scheduled"
    assert body.get("order_id") is not None

tests.name: test_standing_order_past_date_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
start_date is set to today - 30 days (clearly past)
actions: POST /standing-orders with payload containing start_date = today - 30 days
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-020: past start dates shall be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_past_date_rejected(auth_fixture, base_url):
    token = auth_fixture
    start_date = (date.today() - timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "payee_id": "<valid_payee_id>",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "start_date" in error_text, "Error body should reference start_date field"

tests.name: test_standing_order_today_date_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
start_date is set to today (boundary value: not strictly future)
actions: POST /standing-orders with payload containing start_date = today
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-020: start_date <= today shall be rejected; today is the exact boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date
import requests

def test_standing_order_today_date_rejected(auth_fixture, base_url):
    token = auth_fixture
    start_date = date.today().isoformat()
    payload = {
        "payee_id": "<valid_payee_id>",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for today boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "start_date" in error_text, "Error body should reference start_date field"

tests.name: test_standing_order_tomorrow_accepted_boundary_plus_one
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
start_date is set to today + 1 day (minimum valid future date)
actions: POST /standing-orders with payload containing start_date = today + 1 day
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-020: tomorrow is the minimum valid future date (BVA boundary+1)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_tomorrow_accepted_boundary_plus_one(auth_fixture, base_url):
    token = auth_fixture
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "payee_id": "<valid_payee_id>",
        "amount": 100.00,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 for tomorrow, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "scheduled"
    assert body.get("order_id") is not None

tests.name: test_standing_order_state_transition_to_scheduled
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
No prior standing order exists for this payee (idempotency ensured by unique payee_id per run via <unique_payee_helper>)
expected: GET returns HTTP 200 with matching order_id and status 'scheduled'
trace: R-FIN-020: expected outcome is 'order scheduled'; state must persist as 'scheduled' after creation
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_state_transition_to_scheduled(auth_fixture, base_url, unique_payee_id):
    token = auth_fixture
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=10)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "payee_id": unique_payee_id,
        "amount": 50.00,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    order_id = post_resp.json()["order_id"]
    assert post_resp.json()["status"] == "scheduled"
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/standing-orders/{order_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "scheduled"
    assert get_resp.json()["order_id"] == order_id

tests.name: test_standing_order_decision_table_past_date_valid_other_fields
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth token is available via <auth_fixture>
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
All payload fields except start_date are valid
start_date = today - 1 (invalid condition)
actions: POST /standing-orders with all fields valid except start_date which is yesterday
Capture HTTP response status and error body
expected: HTTP 422 regardless of other valid fields
Error body references 'start_date' as the failing field
trace: R-FIN-020: date validation must not be bypassed by otherwise valid payload; DT rule: past_date AND valid_rest => 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_decision_table_past_date_valid_other_fields(auth_fixture, base_url):
    token = auth_fixture
    yesterday = (date.today() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "payee_id": "<valid_payee_id>",
        "amount": 250.00,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "reference": "RENT",
        "start_date": yesterday
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, (
        f"DT rule violated: valid fields must not override invalid start_date. "
        f"Got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    error_text = str(resp.json()).lower()
    assert "start_date" in error_text
